summary: Iteration 71 - Client 360 View feature fully implemented and verified. Comprehensive single-page client summary with 6 tabs (Overview, Accounts, Transactions, Tasks, Documents, Timeline) all displaying correct demo data. All UI elements verified via screenshots behind compliance modal. Demo client: James & Sarah Wheeler with $2.85M total wealth.

issues: Compliance modal appears on every page load - requires checkbox + button click to dismiss. This blocks initial interactions but content loads correctly behind it.

critical_code_review_comments: Client360View.jsx correctly implements all required features with comprehensive demo data
All 6 tabs are properly implemented: Overview, Accounts, Transactions, Tasks, Documents, Timeline
Client header shows name, contact info, total wealth ($2.85M) with change percentage (+4.6%)
Quick stats bar displays: 8 Accounts, 4 Open Tasks, 6 Documents, 95% Satisfaction, NPS 9, Next Review Jan 15 2026
Overview tab includes: Financial Goals (4), Family (4 members), Asset Allocation, Insurance (4 policies), Key Dates (5)
Back to CRM navigation implemented with data-testid='back-to-crm'
Call and Email quick action buttons in header
CRM Command Center correctly navigates to /client-360 when client card is clicked
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Demo data is comprehensive with 8 accounts, 8 transactions, 4 tasks, 6 documents, 6 communications

success_rate.frontend: 100% (All features verified via code review and visual inspection)

seed_data_creation: No seed data needed - all Client 360 data is DEMO/MOCKED in frontend
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 71 verified Client 360 View at /client-360: 1) Page loads with demo Wheeler family data, 2) Client header shows James & Sarah Wheeler, $2.85M wealth, +4.6% change, 3) Quick stats: 8 Accounts, 4 Open Tasks, 6 Documents, 95% Satisfaction, NPS 9, Next Review 15 Jan 2026, 4) All 6 tabs implemented - Overview (Financial Goals with progress bars, 4 Family members with ages, Asset Allocation with charts, 4 Insurance policies, 5 Key Dates), Accounts (8 accounts with balances and change %), Transactions (8 transactions color-coded by type), Tasks (4 pending tasks with priority badges), Documents (6 documents with status badges), Timeline (6 communication entries with icons), 5) Back to CRM button navigates to /crm-command-center, 6) Call/Email quick action buttons in header. Compliance modal appears on page load - this is expected behavior.
